status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 6 / BIOLOGIC OR MIXTURE IDENTITY BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Unresolved PEGylated MGF-derived peptide product family
mechanism_summary: Intended prolongation of an MGF-derived peptide through PEG conjugation; biological behaviour depends on exact peptide sequence and PEG chemistry.
evidence_maturity: Preclinical and analytical domain only; supplier product identity is insufficiently specified and human evidence is not established.
regulatory_headline: UNAPPROVED / CHEMICALLY UNDER-SPECIFIED / ATHLETE PROHIBITION CONTROL REQUIRED
certification_blockers: PEG molecular weight, attachment site and conjugation chemistry
No established human PK, efficacy or safety
closure_class: PEGYLATED MGF E-DOMAIN PRODUCT / SEQUENCE AND CONJUGATION CHEMISTRY
closure_state: BLOCKED — EXACT MGF-DERIVED SEQUENCE, PEG MOLECULAR WEIGHT, ATTACHMENT SITE, CONJUGATION DISTRIBUTION, POTENCY, STERILITY AND HUMAN EVIDENCE UNVERIFIED
authoritative_product_boundary: Primary MGF research concerns defined IGF-1Ec E-domain peptides, including a 24-residue C-terminal peptide, in cells and animal models. Those materials are not the same as an unspecified PEGylated supplier product. PEGylation can materially change molecular mass, distribution, receptor interactions, immunogenicity and assay.
critical_closure_controls: FMP2 is not assumed to be the native 24-residue MGF E-domain peptide
No cell or animal MGF-E evidence transfer to PEG-MGF
PEG chemistry is part of the active product identity
MGF remains within current anti-doping growth-factor control
No muscle, recovery, dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
